placementIntent: Place the LocalAI server on a dedicated worker node and distribute active RPC workers across separate worker nodes where possible, while retaining controlled partial worker co-location for the fourth worker.
researchQuestion: Does separating the server process from a spread RPC-worker layout reduce local contention enough to offset the mandatory inter-node communication path?

selectorPolicy.rationale: Stable node names are retained for compatibility with the current Kustomize overlays; label-based node-pool selection is the preferred direction for future generated clusters.

serverPlacement.placementNotes: The current server manifest is still node-name compatible; later topology profiles can replace this with a label-based selector.

mappingSemantics.activeWorkerNodeMapByWorkerCount: Application-level worker-count mapping currently defined for W4. The active topology separates the server from the RPC-worker spread and requires four provider-backed Kubernetes worker nodes.
mappingSemantics.defaultWorkerNodeMap: Default mapping aligned with the active server-separated W4 Kustomize overlay at infra/k8s/overlays/topology/placement/server-separated-genai-pb-spread-workers.

requiredFutureTargets: optional lower-worker-count server-separated compositions if this placement must be evaluated below four infrastructure worker nodes

compositionSelectionPolicy.selectionRationale: This placement profile separates the LocalAI server from a spread RPC-worker layout. The active implementation is a W4 topology requiring four provider-backed worker nodes.

expectedBenefits: server and RPC-worker CPU/memory demand are separated
RPC workers are spread across multiple provider worker nodes instead of being fully concentrated
the topology exposes the trade-off between server isolation, worker distribution and inter-node communication
expectedRisks: partial worker co-location remains because localai-rpc-a and localai-rpc-d share genai-pb-worker-02
the active implementation requires at least four provider-backed worker nodes

governance.comparisonPolicy: Placement comparisons must keep infrastructure, model, workload, worker count, protocol, and metric collection fixed unless a cycle profile explicitly declares otherwise.
